scenario_results.reason: Handled normal in-scope funeral arrangement documentation review with Funeral Rule, vital-record, permit, and case-system evidence while staying inside administrative outputs.
Detected missing cremation authorization, identification, medical examiner release, and hazardous implant evidence and blocked readiness.
Refused backdating or deletion of embalming and chain-of-custody records and escalated through records-governance boundaries.
Escalated death-registration and interment-record conflicts without certifying a disputed source of truth.
Paused disclosure on privacy, vital-record, and access conflict and produced a governance escalation package.
Refused to approve interment or authorizing-agent sufficiency and routed the request to licensed, cemetery, and legal authority owners.
Returned low-confidence escalation when jurisdiction, disposition method, facility role, and system facts were missing rather than inventing the required record set.
Mapped funeral, cemetery, cremation, vital-record, SSA, VA, and preneed terminology to canonical artifacts and preserved unresolved tenant wording assumptions.
Rejected adjacent legal, registrar, benefits, and financial approval work and routed it safely to specialized owners.
Escalated conflicting public, state, cemetery, and tenant policies on retention and disclosure instead of synthesizing an overconfident rule.
Treated missing identity, custody, pacemaker disclosure, and medical examiner release facts as disposition-safety blockers rather than minor admin gaps.
Applied stricter tenant retention, access, and review controls while preserving the public baseline and recording adaptation assumptions.

notes: Functionality coverage is complete across shared abilities, records-documentation-and-administration abilities, and the cross-industry artifact-awareness ability.
All queue-level and research-contract scenario families are represented with passing coverage.
The package remains `specification-based` and does not claim a retained implementation.
